g.text: Title: Arduino Due blinky - SWD wiring and external LED

g.desc: Title: Arduino Due blinky - SWD wiring and external LED

g.text: Pinout source: Arduino Due datasheet A000062, sections 6.2.6 and 6.5.1

g.desc: Pinout source: Arduino Due datasheet A000062, sections 6.2.6 and 6.5.1

g.text: Flashed with probe-rs via the z5labs/devex flash Dagger module, chip ATSAM3X8E

g.desc: Flashed with probe-rs via the z5labs/devex flash Dagger module, chip ATSAM3X8E

g.text: WARNING - 3.3 V LOGIC. DO NOT CONNECT A 5 V DEBUG PROBE.

g.desc: WARNING - 3.3 V LOGIC. DO NOT CONNECT A 5 V DEBUG PROBE.

g.text: The Arduino Due (Atmel SAM3X8E) runs at 3.3 V, unlike most other Arduino boards.

g.desc: The Arduino Due (Atmel SAM3X8E) runs at 3.3 V, unlike most other Arduino boards.

g.text: 'Unlike most traditional Arduino boards, the Arduino Due board runs at 3.3 V.

g.desc: 'Unlike most traditional Arduino boards, the Arduino Due board runs at 3.3 V.

g.text: Keep in mind the maximum voltage that the I/O pins can tolerate is 3.3 V.

g.desc: Keep in mind the maximum voltage that the I/O pins can tolerate is 3.3 V.

g.text: Applying voltages higher than 3.3 V to any I/O pin could damage the board.'

g.desc: Applying voltages higher than 3.3 V to any I/O pin could damage the board.'

g.text: Use a 3.3 V probe, or one that sets its levels from VTref. Header pin 10 (+3V3) is the

g.desc: Use a 3.3 V probe, or one that sets its levels from VTref. Header pin 10 (+3V3) is the

g.text: board's 3.3 V rail and is what feeds the probe's VTref level reference.

g.desc: board's 3.3 V rail and is what feeds the probe's VTref level reference.

g.text: BEFORE YOU WIRE ANYTHING - CONFIRM PIN 1

g.desc: BEFORE YOU WIRE ANYTHING - CONFIRM PIN 1

g.text: A keyed 2x5 1.27 mm ribbon cable between a standard ARM Cortex Debug probe and this header mates correctly. The connectors are physically compatible, and the reversed

g.desc: A keyed 2x5 1.27 mm ribbon cable between a standard ARM Cortex Debug probe and this header mates correctly. The connectors are physically compatible, and the reversed

g.text: numbering documented above is a labelling convention, not a wiring difference - SEGGER lists the Arduino Due as a standard Cortex-M 10-pin target.

g.desc: numbering documented above is a labelling convention, not a wiring difference - SEGGER lists the Arduino Due as a standard Cortex-M 10-pin target.

g.text: If you instead hand-wire flying leads, the two numbering conventions are exact opposites, and following the wrong one connects +3V3 to nRESET and vice versa. Identify pin 1 on

g.desc: If you instead hand-wire flying leads, the two numbering conventions are exact opposites, and following the wrong one connects +3V3 to nRESET and vice versa. Identify pin 1 on

g.text: the physical board (silkscreen dot / square pad) and confirm it with a continuity check against a known GND pin before applying power.

g.desc: the physical board (silkscreen dot / square pad) and confirm it with a continuity check against a known GND pin before applying power.

g.text: Flashing over SWD ERASES THE ARDUINO BOOTLOADER. This is expected and reversible: recover with the ERASE button followed by a SAM-BA bootloader reflash.

g.desc: Flashing over SWD ERASES THE ARDUINO BOOTLOADER. This is expected and reversible: recover with the ERASE button followed by a SAM-BA bootloader reflash.

g.text: R1 = 1 k, giving roughly 1 mA through a red LED - dim but plainly visible

g.desc: R1 = 1 k, giving roughly 1 mA through a red LED - dim but plainly visible

g.text: Do NOT reach for the reflexive 220 ohm. PB26 falls in the SAM3X datasheet's

g.desc: Do NOT reach for the reflexive 220 ohm. PB26 falls in the SAM3X datasheet's

g.text: Group 2 (PB[25-31]), whose source limit is IOH = -3 mA at VOH = VDDIO - 0.4 V,

g.desc: Group 2 (PB[25-31]), whose source limit is IOH = -3 mA at VOH = VDDIO - 0.4 V,

g.text: not the -15 mA that Group 1 pins allow. At 220 ohm the LED would draw roughly

g.desc: not the -15 mA that Group 1 pins allow. At 220 ohm the LED would draw roughly

g.text: 4 mA, outside that spec. 470 ohm (about 2 mA) is the practical floor if you

g.desc: 4 mA, outside that spec. 470 ohm (about 2 mA) is the practical floor if you

g.text: Source: SAM3X / SAM3A datasheet, table 45-2 with notes 2 and 3.

g.desc: Source: SAM3X / SAM3A datasheet, table 45-2 with notes 2 and 3.

g.text: WHERE TO WIRE IT - MIND THE +5V PIN NEXT DOOR

g.desc: WHERE TO WIRE IT - MIND THE +5V PIN NEXT DOOR

g.text: Use the D22-D53 header, even-numbered (LHS) row. Counting along that row:

g.desc: Use the D22-D53 header, even-numbered (LHS) row. Counting along that row:

g.text: position 2 = D22 <-- LED anode side, through R1

g.desc: position 2 = D22 <-- LED anode side, through R1

g.text: D22 is the SECOND pin in that row, and the pin immediately next to it is

g.desc: D22 is the SECOND pin in that row, and the pin immediately next to it is

g.text: +5V. Miscounting by one position feeds 5 V into a board whose I/O pins

g.desc: +5V. Miscounting by one position feeds 5 V into a board whose I/O pins

g.text: tolerate 3.3 V. Count twice, and confirm GND with a continuity check,

g.desc: tolerate 3.3 V. Count twice, and confirm GND with a continuity check,

g.text: Source: Arduino Due datasheet A000062, section 6.2.4.

g.desc: Source: Arduino Due datasheet A000062, section 6.2.4.

g.text: WHY AN EXTERNAL LED, AND NOT THE ON-BOARD ONE

g.desc: WHY AN EXTERNAL LED, AND NOT THE ON-BOARD ONE

g.text: This LED is wired by you. The on-board D13 'L' LED is deliberately

g.desc: This LED is wired by you. The on-board D13 'L' LED is deliberately

g.text: NOT used and is not driven by this firmware.

g.desc: NOT used and is not driven by this firmware.

g.text: An on-board LED blinking is weak evidence: bootloader activity, or a

g.desc: An on-board LED blinking is weak evidence: bootloader activity, or a

g.text: watchdog reset loop, can make the board blink on its own. Issue #3

g.desc: watchdog reset loop, can make the board blink on its own. Issue #3

g.text: calls out that exact failure mode - if the watchdog is not disabled,

g.desc: calls out that exact failure mode - if the watchdog is not disabled,

g.text: the board resets every few seconds and 'the blink looks like a boot

g.desc: the board resets every few seconds and 'the blink looks like a boot

g.text: loop'. Those two cases are indistinguishable by eye on D13.

g.desc: loop'. Those two cases are indistinguishable by eye on D13.

g.text: PB26 / D22 has no on-board LED, no boot-time role, and no peripheral

g.desc: PB26 / D22 has no on-board LED, no boot-time role, and no peripheral

g.text: alternate function in the Arduino variant table. If it blinks at a

g.desc: alternate function in the Arduino variant table. If it blinks at a

g.text: steady 1 Hz, the firmware is running and toggling a pin.

g.desc: steady 1 Hz, the firmware is running and toggling a pin.

g.text: Active high: driving PB26 high lights the LED.

g.desc: Active high: driving PB26 high lights the LED.

g.text: JTAG / SWD HEADER PIN NUMBERING - READ BEFORE WIRING

g.desc: JTAG / SWD HEADER PIN NUMBERING - READ BEFORE WIRING

g.text: The Due's on-board 10-pin header carries the standard ARM Cortex Debug signal set,

g.desc: The Due's on-board 10-pin header carries the standard ARM Cortex Debug signal set,

g.text: but Arduino numbers the connector from the OPPOSITE END to the ARM standard:

g.desc: but Arduino numbers the connector from the OPPOSITE END to the ARM standard:

g.text: Arduino pin N == ARM Cortex Debug pin (11 - N)

g.desc: Arduino pin N == ARM Cortex Debug pin (11 - N)

g.text: Arduino | Signal | ARM Cortex Debug pin

g.desc: Arduino | Signal | ARM Cortex Debug pin

g.text: Source: Arduino Due datasheet A000062, sections 6.2.6 and 6.5.1.

g.desc: Source: Arduino Due datasheet A000062, sections 6.2.6 and 6.5.1.

g.desc: Arduino Due - on-board JTAG / SWD header

g.text: External LED on PB26 / D22 - the blink indicator

g.desc: External LED on PB26 / D22 - the blink indicator
